description: engram: decision capture + context injection via hooks — session lifecycle, code change nudges, signal validation, compaction safety, automatic context retrieval

hooks.prompt: Review this session. You MUST respond with ONLY valid JSON, no other text. If the agent made significant code changes (new features, architecture changes, refactors, dependency changes, non-trivial bug fixes) via Write or Edit tools, check whether at least one NEW .engram/signals/ file was also created via Write during this session. Files inside .engram/ themselves don't count as code changes. Trivial changes (typo fixes, formatting, comment-only edits, test-only changes, changes to README.md, CHANGELOG.md, LICENSE, or files in a docs/ directory) are exempt. Note: SKILL.md files, plugin manifests, schema files, and hook scripts are functional code, NOT documentation. If significant code changes exist but no .engram/signals/ file was written, respond {"ok": false, "reason": "Significant changes need a decision signal — use @engram:capture to write .engram/signals/decision-{slug}.md"}. Otherwise respond {"ok": true}.

A file was just written or edited. You MUST respond with ONLY valid JSON, no other text. Check if this was a significant code change (new feature, architecture change, refactor, dependency change, non-trivial bug fix). Respond {"ok": true} if ANY of these apply: (1) the file is inside .engram/, (2) the change is test-only, (3) the change is formatting/whitespace-only, (4) the file is pure project documentation (README.md, CHANGELOG.md, LICENSE, or files inside a docs/ directory). Note: SKILL.md files, plugin manifests, schema files, and hook scripts are functional code, NOT documentation — do not skip these. If the change IS significant and no .engram/signals/ file has been written this session, respond {"ok": true, "reason": "Consider recording this decision: @engram:capture can write a signal file."}. Only nudge ONCE per session; if already nudged, respond {"ok": true}.

Check the file path being written or edited. You MUST respond with ONLY valid JSON, no other text. If the path does NOT target .engram/signals/ or .engram/_private/, respond {"ok": true}. If the path DOES target .engram/signals/ or .engram/_private/, validate ALL of the following: (1) Filename matches pattern {type}-{slug}.md where type is one of: decision, finding, issue. (2) File content starts with --- frontmatter delimiter on line 1. (3) File content contains a closing --- frontmatter delimiter. (4) Frontmatter contains a date: field. (5) Body after frontmatter contains at least one line of content. If ALL validations pass, respond {"ok": true}. If ANY validation fails, respond {"ok": false, "reason": "description of which validation failed and how to fix it"}.

Review what this subagent accomplished. You MUST respond with ONLY valid JSON, no other text. If the subagent made architectural recommendations, design decisions, or suggested significant technical direction, respond {"ok": true, "reason": "This subagent made recommendations that may warrant a decision signal. Consider @engram:capture. Check related past decisions with @engram:query."}. If the subagent only performed routine tasks, respond {"ok": true}.

Context is about to be compacted. You MUST respond with ONLY valid JSON, no other text. Scan the conversation for decisions discussed but NOT yet recorded as .engram/signals/ files. If you find unrecorded decisions, respond {"ok": true, "reason": "Before compaction, these decisions appear unrecorded: [list]. Consider @engram:capture."}. If all decisions have signal files or none were discussed, respond {"ok": true}. Always respond ok:true — never block compaction.

You MUST respond with ONLY valid JSON, no other text. Check if the user's message contains definitive decision language: 'let's go with', 'we decided', 'switching to', 'going with', 'the decision is', 'we'll use', 'agreed on', 'settled on'. Ignore hypotheticals: 'should we', 'what if', 'maybe', 'could we'. If decision language is detected AND no @engram:capture nudge has been given this conversation, respond {"ok": true, "reason": "That sounds like a decision. Consider recording it with @engram:capture."}. Only nudge ONCE. Also independently check if the user asks about past decisions ('why did we', 'what was decided', 'remind me'). If so, respond {"ok": true, "reason": "Relevant past signals may exist — consider @engram:query."}. If neither applies, respond {"ok": true}.

You MUST respond with ONLY valid JSON, no other text. Check if .engram/signals/ contains issue-*.md files. Count those without 'status: resolved' in frontmatter. If open issues exist and at least one is older than 7 days (based on date: field), respond {"ok": true, "reason": "You have N open engram issues. Use @engram:query to review them."}. Otherwise respond {"ok": true}.
